title: MAPriors: Browser-Based Meta-Analytic Predictive Priors and Dynamic Borrowing Engine
author: Mahmood Ahmad
body: Can meta-analytic predictive priors and dynamic borrowing be made accessible through a browser-based tool without requiring R or Stan expertise? Eight historical Crohn disease placebo-arm studies totaling 960 patients from the Neuenschwander canonical dataset were analyzed alongside oncology ORR and ulcerative colitis CDAI built-in examples. MAPriors, a single-file HTML application of 3,925 lines, implements Bayesian hierarchical modeling with robust mixture priors, power priors, commensurate priors, prior evolution animation, and operating characteristics simulation, with TruthCert SHA-256 audit provenance. The Crohn dataset produced a MAP prior mean of 17.7% (95% credible interval 11.2-25.8%) with Morita effective sample size of 959 patients under near-zero heterogeneity. Operating characteristics confirmed nominal type I error near 5% and power gains of 10-15 percentage points over vague-prior designs, with sample size savings of 20-35% in the control arm. This is the first browser-based MAP prior engine, validated by 40 Selenium tests with WebR cross-validation and regulatory reporting. A limitation is the approximate REML inference rather than full Hamiltonian Monte Carlo.

sentences.text: Can meta-analytic predictive priors and dynamic borrowing be made accessible through a browser-based tool without requiring R or Stan expertise?
Eight historical Crohn disease placebo-arm studies totaling 960 patients from the Neuenschwander canonical dataset were analyzed alongside oncology ORR and ulcerative colitis CDAI built-in examples.

MAPriors, a single-file HTML application of 3,925 lines, implements Bayesian hierarchical modeling with robust mixture priors, power priors, commensurate priors, prior evolution animation, and operating characteristics simulation, with TruthCert SHA-256 audit provenance.
The Crohn dataset produced a MAP prior mean of 17.7% (95% credible interval 11.2-25.8%) with Morita effective sample size of 959 patients under near-zero heterogeneity.
Operating characteristics confirmed nominal type I error near 5% and power gains of 10-15 percentage points over vague-prior designs, with sample size savings of 20-35% in the control arm.
This is the first browser-based MAP prior engine, validated by 40 Selenium tests with WebR cross-validation and regulatory reporting.
A limitation is the approximate REML inference rather than full Hamiltonian Monte Carlo.

notes.data: Crohn disease placebo (Neuenschwander 2010), UC CDAI, oncology ORR (built-in)

checks.name: single paragraph
checks.detail: Body must not contain blank-line paragraph breaks.

checks.name: no headings
checks.detail: No markdown headings allowed in body.

checks.name: no links
checks.detail: No links or DOI links allowed in body.

checks.name: result sentence has interval
checks.detail: Sentence 4 should include a quantitative result such as a percentage, count, or interval.

checks.name: result sentence has estimand
checks.detail: Sentence 4 should name an effect measure or test metric.

checks.name: boundary sentence present
checks.detail: Sentence 7 should express a limitation, harm, or scope boundary.
